title: Node HTTP Primitives, Connections, Protocols, Timeouts, and Shutdown
description: A Node server inherits defaults without understanding header, request, keep-alive, upgrade, idle-socket, HTTP/2, or graceful-close behavior. Learners cumulatively produce Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace IncomingMessage and ServerResponse as streams with header commitment, body ownership, backpressure, abort, error, and close lifetimes.
Configure requestTimeout, headersTimeout, keepAliveTimeout, keepAliveTimeoutBuffer, maxHeadersCount, and maximum header size from a threat and latency budget.
Distinguish HTTP/1.1, Node HTTP/2 compatibility behavior, ALPN, TLS, upgrades, CONNECT, and the explicit external boundary for HTTP/3.
